executiveSummary.text: TomCo is deep in the category of AIM micro-cap optionality where the listed equity often behaves more like a perpetual financing stub than an ownership stake in a developing business. At 0.0330p, with a live range of 0.0310p to 0.0332p, the market is effectively telling you the equity has very little residual credibility.Bottom line: SELL — SPECULATIVE. This is not a serious long-term holding candidate unless there is a genuinely transformative catalyst, and even then the dilution risk is severe

businessModel.text: TomCo is essentially a development-stage energy/project shell story. For practical purposes, valuation depends on future financing and speculative project outcomes rather than on cash-generating operations.That creates a very poor risk/reward setup for ordinary shareholders because any path forward usually requires more capital and more dilution.

thesisEvaluation.text: Bull (15%): a corporate or project surprise creates a short sharp speculative rerating.Base (45%): the shares drift with negligible fundamental support.Bear (40%): further dilution or stagnation destroys more value.Weighted conviction: 22/100.

bull.scenario: Bull (15%): a corporate or project surprise creates a short sharp speculative rerating.

base.scenario: Base (45%): the shares drift with negligible fundamental support.

bear.scenario: Bear (40%): further dilution or stagnation destroys more value.

keyRisks.text: Dilution risk: the dominant risk. No operating base: little to anchor valuation. Liquidity and trading risk: tiny moves can be meaningless and illiquid.

risks.risk: No operating base: little to anchor valuation.
Liquidity and trading risk: tiny moves can be meaningless and illiquid.

whoShouldOwn.text: Should own: almost nobody beyond punters knowingly trading lottery-ticket risk. Should avoid: anyone treating public equities like investments rather than punts.

recommendation.text: SELL — SPECULATIVE at 0.0330p. This is a stub, not a business-quality setup.
